section-59.title: ` Provision of accommodation by voluntary organisations.

section-59-1.content: Where a voluntary organisation provide accommodation for a child, they shall do so by—

section-59-1-aa.content: maintaining him ina children's home in respect of which a person is registered under Part 2 of the Care Standards Act 2000or Part 1 of the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016;

section-59-1-f.content: making such other arrangements (subject to subsection (3)) as seem appropriate to them.

additional_content: a family;
a relative of his; or
any other suitable person,
on such terms as to payment by the organisation and otherwise as the organisation may determine(subject to section 49 of the Children Act 2004);

section-59-1A.content: Where under subsection (1)(aa) avoluntary organisationmaintains a child in a home provided, equipped and maintained byan appropriate national authorityunder section 82(5), it shall do so on such terms asthat national authoritymay from time to time determine.

section-59-2.content: Theappropriate national authoritymay make regulations as to the placing of children with foster parents by voluntary organisations ... .

section-59-3.content: Theappropriate national authoritymay make regulations as to the arrangements which may be made under subsection (1)(f) ... .

section-59-3A.content: Regulationsmade in relation to Englandunder subsection (2) or (3) may in particular make provision which (with any necessary modifications) is similar to that which may be made under section 22C by virtue of any of paragraphs 12B, 12E and 12F of Schedule 2.

section-59-3B.content: Regulations made in relation to Wales under subsection (2) or (3) may in particular make provision which (with any necessary modifications) is similar to that which may be made under sections 81 or 87 of the Social Services and Well-being (Wales) Act 2014, including provision which may be made under section 87 in accordance with the examples given in sections 89, 92 and 93 of that Act.

section-59-4.content: Theappropriate national authoritymay make regulations requiring any voluntary organisation who are providing accommodation for a child—

section-59-4-b.content: to consider any representations (including any complaint) made to them by any person falling within a prescribed class of person,

section-59-4.additional_content: in accordance with the provisions of the regulations.

section-59-5A.content: Regulationsmade in relation to Englandunder subsection (4) may, in particular—

section-59-5B.content: Regulations made in relation to Wales under subsection (4) may in particular make provision which (with any necessary modifications) is similar to that which may be made under sections 99, 100 or 102 of the Social Services and Well-being (Wales) Act 2014.

section-59-6.content: Regulations under subsections (2) to (4) may provide that any person who, without reasonable excuse, contravenes or fails to comply with a regulation shall be guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 4 on the standard scale.

section-59-7.content: In this Part “appropriate national authority” means—

section-59-7-a.content: in relation to England, the Secretary of State; and

section-59-7-b.content: in relation to Wales, the Welsh Ministers.

section-60-3.content: In this Act “voluntary home” means a children’s home which is carried on by a voluntary organisation but does not include a community home.

section-60-4.content: Schedule 5 shall have effect for the purpose of supplementing the provisions of this Part.

section-61-1.content: Where a child is accommodated by or on behalf of a voluntary organisation, it shall be the duty of the organisation—

section-61-1-a.content: to safeguard and promote his welfare;

section-61-1-b.content: to make such use of the services and facilities available for children cared for by their own parents as appears to the organisation reasonable in his case; and

section-61-1-c.content: to advise, assist and befriend him with a view to promoting his welfare when he ceases to be so accommodated.

section-61-2.content: Before making any decision with respect to any such child the organisation shall, so far as is reasonably practicable, ascertain the wishes and feelings of—

section-61-2-c.content: any person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him; and

section-61-2-d.content: any other person whose wishes and feelings the organisation consider to be relevant,

section-61-2.additional_content: regarding the matter to be decided.

section-61-3.content: In making any such decision the organisation shall give due consideration—

section-61-3-a.content: having regard to the child’s age and understanding, to such wishes and feelings of his as they have been able to ascertain;

section-61-3-b.content: to such other wishes and feelings mentioned in subsection (2) as they have been able to ascertain; and

section-61-3-c.content: to the child’s religious persuasion, racial origin and cultural and linguistic background.

section-62-1.content: Every local authority shall satisfy themselves that any voluntary organisation providing accommodation—

section-62-1-a.content: within the authority’s area for any child; or

section-62-1-b.content: outside that area for any child on behalf of the authority,

section-62-1.additional_content: are satisfactorily safeguarding and promoting the welfare of the children so provided with accommodation.

section-62-2.content: Every local authority shall arrange for children who are accommodated within their area by or on behalf of voluntary organisations to be visited, from time to time, in the interests of their welfare.

section-62-3-a.content: requiring every child who is accommodated within a local authority’s area, by or on behalf of a voluntary organisation, to be visited by an officer of the authority—

section-62-3-b.content: imposing requirements which must be met by any local authority, or officer of a local authority, carrying out functions under this section.

additional_content: in prescribed circumstances; and
on specified occasions or within specified periods; and

section-62-4.content: Subsection (2) does not apply in relation to community homes.

section-62-5.content: Where a local authority are not satisfied that the welfare of any child who is accommodated by or on behalf of a voluntary organisation is being satisfactorily safeguarded or promoted they shall—

section-62-5-a.content: unless they consider that it would not be in the best interests of the child, take such steps as are reasonably practicable to secure that the care and accommodation of the child is undertaken by—

section-62-5-b.content: consider the extent to which (if at all) they should exercise any of their functions with respect to the child.

additional_content: a parent of his;
any person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him; or
a relative of his; and

section-62-6.content: Any person authorised by a local authority may, for the purpose of enabling the authority to discharge their duties under this section—

section-62-6-a.content: enter, at any reasonable time, and inspect any premises in which children are being accommodated as mentioned in subsection (1) or (2);

section-62-6-c.content: require any person to furnish him with such records of a kind required to be kept by regulations made undersection 22 of the Care Standards Act 2000or section 20 of the Health and Social Care Act 2008, or section 27 of the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016 (anaw 2)(in whatever form they are held), or allow him to inspect such records, as he may at any time direct.

section-62-7.content: Any person exercising the power conferred by subsection (6) shall, if asked to do so, produce some duly authenticated document showing his authority to do so.

section-62-8.content: Any person authorised to exercise the power to inspect records conferred by subsection (6)—

section-62-8-a.content: shall be entitled at any reasonable time to have access to, and inspect and check the operation of, any computer and any associated apparatus or material which is or has been in use in connection with the records in question; and

additional_content: the person by whom or on whose behalf the computer is or has been so used; or
any person having charge of, or otherwise concerned with the operation of, the computer, apparatus or material,
to afford him such assistance as he may reasonably require.

section-62-9.content: Any person who intentionally obstructs another in the exercise of any power conferred by subsection (6) or (8) shall be guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 3 on the standard scale.

section-62-10.content: This section does not apply in relation to any voluntary organisation which is an institution within the further education sector, as defined in section 91 of theFurther and Higher Education Act 1992,a 16 to 19 Academyor a school.

section-63-11.content: Schedule 6 shall have effect with respect toprivatechildren’s homes.

section-63-12.content: Schedule 7 shall have effect for the purpose of setting out the circumstances in which a person may foster more than three childrenwithout being treated—

section-63-12-a.content: for the purposes of this Act and the Care Standards Act 2000,as carrying on a children’s home; and

section-63-12-b.content: for the purposes of the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016, as providing a care home service within the meaning of Part 1 of that Act.

section-64.title: Welfare of children in children’s homes.

section-64-1.content: Where a child is accommodated in aprivatechildren’s home, it shall be the duty of the person carrying on the home to—

section-64-1-a.content: safeguard and promote the child’s welfare;

section-64-1-b.content: make such use of the services and facilities available for children cared for by their own parents as appears to that person reasonable in the case of the child; and

section-64-1-c.content: advise, assist and befriend him with a view to promoting his welfare when he ceases to be so accommodated.

section-64-2.content: Before making any decision with respect to any such child the person carrying on the home shall, so far as is reasonably practicable, ascertain the wishes and feelings of—

section-64-2-c.content: any other person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him; and

section-64-2-d.content: any person whose wishes and feelings the person carrying on the home considers to be relevant,

section-64-2.additional_content: regarding the matter to be decided.

section-64-3.content: In making any such decision the person concerned shall give due consideration—

section-64-3-a.content: having regard to the child’s age and understanding, to such wishes and feelings of his as he has been able to ascertain;

section-64-3-b.content: to such other wishes and feelings mentioned in subsection (2) as he has been able to ascertain; and

section-64-3-c.content: to the child’s religious persuasion, racial origin and cultural and linguistic background.

section-64-4.content: Section 62, except subsection (4), shall apply in relation to any person who is carrying on aprivatechildren’s home as it applies in relation to any voluntary organisation.

section-65.title: 2 Persons disqualified from carrying on, or being employed in children’s homes.

section-65-1.content: A person who is disqualified (under section 68) from fostering a child privately shall not carry on, or be otherwise concerned in the management of, or have any financial interest in a children’s homein Walesunless he has—

section-65-1-a.content: disclosed tothe appropriate authoritythe fact that he is so disqualified; and

section-65-2.content: No person shall employ a person who is so disqualified in a children’s homein Walesunless he has—

section-65-2-a.content: disclosed tothe appropriate authoritythe fact that that person is so disqualified; and

section-65-3.content: Wherethe appropriate authority refuses to give its consent under this section, itshall inform the applicant by a written notice which states—

section-65-3-b.content: the applicant’s right to appeal under section 65A against the refusal to theFirst-tier Tribunal

section-65-3-c.content: the time within which he may do so.

section-65-4.content: Any person who contravenes subsection(A1), (A2),(1) or (2) shall be guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to a fine not exceeding level 5 on the standard scale or to both.

section-65-5.content: Where a person contravenes subsection(A2) or(2) he shall not be guilty of an offence if he proves that he did not know, and had no reasonable grounds for believing, that the person whom he was employing was disqualified under section 68.

section-65-6.content: In this section and section 65A “appropriate authority” means—

section-65-6-a.content: in relation to England, theHer Majesty's Chief Inspector of Education, Children's Services and Skills; and

section-65-6-b.content: in relation to Wales, the National Assembly for Wales.

section-65A.title: Appeal against refusal of authority to give consent under section 65.
section-65A.content:
(1)An appeal against a decision of an appropriate authority under section 65 shall lie to theFirst-tier Tribunal.
(2)On an appeal the Tribunal may confirm the authority’s decision or direct it to give the consent in question.

section-66-1-a.content: “a privately fostered child” means a child who is under the age of sixteen and who is cared for, and provided with accommodationin their own homeby, someone other than—

section-66-1-b.content: “to foster a child privately” means to look after the child in circumstances in which he is a privately fostered child as defined by this section.

additional_content: a parent of his;
a person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him; or
a relative of his; and

section-66-2.content: A child is not privately fostered child if the person caring for and accommodating him—

section-66-2-a.content: has done so for a period a period of less than 28 days; and

section-66-2-b.content: does not intend to do so for any longer period.

section-66-3-b.content: the exceptions made by paragraph 1 to 5 of Schedule 8.

section-66-4.content: In the case of a child who is disabled, subsection (1)(a) shall have effect as if for “sixteen”there were substituted “eighteen”.

section-66-4A.content: The Secretary of State may by regulations make provision as to the circumstances in which a person who provides accommodation to a child is, or is not, to be treated as providing him with accommodation in the person’s own home.

section-66-5.content: Schedule 8 shall have effect for the purposes of supplementing the provision made by this Part.

section-67-1.content: It shall be the duty of every local authority to satisfy themselves that the welfare of children who areor are proposed to beprivately fostered within their area is beingor will besatisfactorily safeguarded and promoted and to secure that such advice is given to thoseconcerned withthem as appears to the authority to be needed.

section-67-2.content: The Secretary of State may make regulations—

section-67-2-a.content: requiring every child who is privately fostered within a local authority’s area to be visited by an officer of the authority—

additional_content: in prescribed circumstances; and
on specified occasions or within specified periods; and

section-67-2A.content: Regulations under subsection (2)(b) may impose requirements as to the action to be taken by a local authority for the purposes of discharging their duty under subsection (1) where they have received notification of a proposal that a child be privately fostered.

section-67-3.content: Where any person who is authorised by a local authority to visitfor the purposehas reasonable cause to believe that—

section-67-3-a.content: any privately fostered child is being accommodated in premises within the authority’s area; or

section-67-3-b.content: it is proposed to accommodate any such child in any such premises,

section-67-3.additional_content: he may at any reasonable time inspect those premises and any children there.

section-67-4.content: Any person exercising the power under subsection (3) shall, if so required, produce some duly authenticated document showing his authority to do so.

section-67-5.content: Where a local authority are not satisfied that the welfare of any child who isor is proposed to beprivately fostered within their area is beingor will besatisfactorily safeguarded or promoted they shall—

section-67-5-a.content: unless they consider that it would not be in the best interests of the child, take such steps as are reasonably practicable to secure that the care and accommodation of the child is undertaken by—

section-67-5-b.content: consider the extent to which (if at all) they should exercise any of their functions under this Act with respect to the child.

additional_content: a parent of his;
any person who is not a parent of his but who has
parental responsibility for him; or
a relative of his; and

section-67-6.content: The Secretary of State may make regulations requiring a local authority to monitor the way in which the authority discharge their functions under this Part (and the regulations may in particular require the authority to appoint an officer for that purpose).

section-68.title: Persons disqualified from being private foster parents.

section-68-1.content: Unless he has disclosed the fact to the appropriate local authority and obtained their written consent, a person shall not foster a child privately if he is disqualified from doing so by regulations made by the Secretary of State for the purposes of this section.

section-68-2.content: The regulations may, in particular, provide for a person to be so disqualified where—

section-68-2-a.content: an order of a kind specified in the regulations has been made at any time with respect to him;

section-68-2-b.content: an order of a kind so specified has been made at any time with respect to any child who has been in his care;

section-68-2-c.content: a requirement of a kind so specified has been imposed at any time with respect to any such child, under or by virtue of any enactment;

section-68-2-d.content: he has been convicted of any offence of a kind so specified, or. . . discharged absolutely or conditionally for any such offence;

section-68-2-e.content: a prohibition has been imposed on him at any time under section 69 or under any other specified enactment;

section-68-2-f.content: his rights and powers with respect to a child have at any time been vested in a specified authority under a specified enactment.

section-68-2A.content: A conviction in respect of which a probation order was made before 1st October 1992 (which would not otherwise be treated as a conviction) is to be treated as a conviction for the purposes of subsection (2)(d).

section-68-3.content: Unless he has disclosed the fact to the appropriate local authority and obtained their written consent, a person shall not foster a child privately if—

section-68-3-a.content: he lives in the same household as a person who is himself prevented from fostering a child by subsection (1); or

section-68-3-b.content: he lives in a household at which any such person is employed.

section-68-3A.content: A person shall not foster a child privately if—

section-68-4.content: Where an authority refuse to give their consent under this section, they shall inform the applicant by a written notice which states—

section-68-4-b.content: the applicant’s right under paragraph 8 of Schedule 8 to appeal against the refusal; and

section-68-4-c.content: the time within which he may do so.

items: “ the appropriate authority ” means the local authority within whose area it is proposed to foster the child in question; and
“ enactment ” means any enactment having effect, at any time, in any part of the United Kingdom.

section-69-1.content: This section applies where a person—

section-69-1-a.content: proposes to foster a child privately; or

section-69-2.content: Where the local authority for the area within which the child is proposed to be, or is being, fostered are of the opinion that—

section-69-2-a.content: he is not a suitable person to foster a child;

section-69-2-b.content: the premises in which the child will be, or is being, accommodated are not suitable; or

section-69-2-c.content: it would be prejudicial to the welfare of the child for him to be, or continue to be accommodated by that person in those premises,

section-69-2.additional_content: the authority may impose a prohibition on him under subsection (3).

section-69-3.content: A prohibition imposed on any person under this subsection may prohibit him from fostering privately—

section-69-3-a.content: any child in any premises within the area of the local authority; or

section-69-3-b.content: any child in premises specified in the prohibition.

section-69-3-c.content: a child identified in the prohibition, in premises specified in the prohibition.

section-69-4.content: A local authority who have imposed a prohibition on any person under subsection (3) may, if they think fit, cancel the prohibition—

section-69-4-b.content: on an application made by that person,

section-69-4.additional_content: if they are satisfied that the prohibition is no longer justified.

section-69-5.content: Where a local authority impose a requirement on any person under paragraph 6 of Schedule 8, they may also impose a prohibition on him under subsection (3).

section-69-6.content: Any prohibition imposed by virtue of subsection (5) shall not have effect unless—

section-69-6-a.content: the time specified for compliance with the requirement has expired; and

section-69-6-b.content: the requirement has not been compiled with.

section-69-7.content: A prohibition imposed under this section shall be imposed by notice in writing addressed to the person on whom it is imposed and informing him of—

section-69-7-a.content: the reason for imposing the prohibition;

section-69-7-b.content: his right under paragraph 8 of Schedule 8 to appeal against the prohibition; and

section-69-7-c.content: the time within which he may do so.

section-70-1.content: A person shall be guilty of an offence if—

section-70-1-a.content: being required, under any provision made by or under this Part, to give any notice or information—

section-70-1-b.content: he refuses to allow a privately fostered child to be visited by a duly authorised officer of a local authority;

section-70-1-c.content: he intentionally obstructs another in the exercise of the power conferred by section 67(3);

section-70-1-e.content: he fails without reasonable excuse to comply with any requirement imposed by a local authority under this Part;

section-70-1-f.content: he accommodates a privately fostered child in any premises in contravention of a prohibition imposed by a local authority under this Part;

section-70-1-g.content: he knowingly causes to be published, or publishes, an advertisement which he knows contravenes paragraph 10 of Schedule 8.

additional_content: he fails without reasonable excuse to give the notice within the time specified in that provision; or
he fails without reasonable excuse to give the information within a reasonable time; or
he makes, or causes or procures another person to make, any statement in the notice or information which he knows to be false or misleading in a material particular;

section-70-2.content: Where a person contravenes section 68(3), he shall not be guilty of an offence under this section if he proves that he did not know, and had no reasonable ground for believing, that any person to whom section 68(1) applied was living or employed in the premises in question.

section-70-3.content: A person guilty of an offence under subsection (1)(a) shall be liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 5 on the standard scale.

section-70-4.content: A person guilty of an offence under subsection (1)(b), (c) or (g) shall be liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 3 on the standard scale.

section-70-5.content: A person guilty of an offence under subsection (1)(d) or (f) shall be liable on summary conviction to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months, or to a fine not exceeding level 5 on the standard scale, or to both.

section-70-6.content: A person guilty of an offence under subsection (1)(e) shall be liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 4 on the standard scale.

section-70-7.content: If any person who is required, under any provision of this Part, to give a notice fails to give the notice within the time specified in that provision, proceedings for the offence may be brought at any time within six months from the date when evidence of the offence came to the knowledge of the local authority.

section-70-8.content: Subsection (7) is not affected by anything in section 127(1) of the Magistrates’ Courts Act 1980 (time limit for proceedings).
